documentation.value:
```kotlin
val name: Type = value
```
Declares a read-only (immutable) variable.

```kotlin
var name: Type = value
```
Declares a mutable variable.

```kotlin
sealed class Result {
    data class Success(val data: String) : Result()
    data class Error(val msg: String) : Result()
}
```
Declares a sealed class.

```kotlin
data class Name(val prop: Type)
```
A class primarily used to hold data. Automatically generates equals(), hashCode(), toString(), copy(), and componentN().
Marks a class or member as open for inheritance/overriding. Classes in Kotlin are final by default.
Marks a member as overriding a superclass or interface member.

```kotlin
when (value) {
    condition -> result
    else -> default
}
```
Pattern matching expression, replacement for switch.
Alternative branch in an if expression.
Returns a value from a function. Can use labels for non-local returns.
Terminates the nearest enclosing loop.
Proceeds to the next step of the nearest enclosing loop.

```kotlin
throw Exception("message")
```
Throws an exception.
Catches exceptions thrown in a try block.
Block that always executes after try/catch, regardless of outcome.
Checks whether an object is of a given type. Smart cast applies after the check.
Unsafe type cast. Use `as?` for safe cast that returns null on failure.
Used in for loops and to check containment.
Marks a type parameter as covariant (producer).
Used for class delegation or delegated properties.
Specifies upper bound constraints for generic type parameters.
Marks a function as suspendable for use in coroutines.
Requests the compiler to inline the function and its lambda parameters at call sites.
Allows calling a function with infix notation (without dot and parentheses).
Marks a function as an operator overload.
Marks a function as tail-recursive, enabling compiler optimization.
Marks a declaration as implemented externally (e.g., in native code).

```kotlin
annotation class MyAnnotation
```
Declares an annotation class.
Forbids non-local returns in a lambda passed to an inline function.
Prevents inlining of a lambda parameter in an inline function.
Makes a type parameter of an inline function available at runtime.
Allows passing a variable number of arguments to a function parameter.

```kotlin
typealias Name = ExistingType
```
Creates an alias for an existing type.
Imports a class, function, or property from another package.
Declares the package of the current file.
Visible only inside the file or class containing the declaration.
Visible in the class and its subclasses.
Visible within the same module.
Visible everywhere. This is the default visibility in Kotlin.
Allows initializing a non-null property outside of a constructor.
Marks a property as a compile-time constant. Must be a top-level or companion object val of primitive or String type.
Declares a platform-specific API expected to be implemented in actual modules.
Provides the platform-specific implementation for an expect declaration.
Refers to the current receiver.
Refers to the superclass implementation.
Represents the null reference.
Boolean true value.
Boolean false value.

```kotlin
fun println(message: Any?): Unit
```
Prints the given message and a newline to standard output.

```kotlin
fun print(message: Any?): Unit
```
Prints the given message to standard output without a newline.

```kotlin
fun readLine(): String?
```
Reads a line from standard input. Returns null if EOF.

```kotlin
fun <T> listOf(vararg elements: T): List<T>
```
Returns a new read-only list of given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> mutableListOf(vararg elements: T): MutableList<T>
```
Returns a new MutableList with the given elements.

fun <K, V> mutableMapOf(vararg pairs: Pair<K, V>): MutableMap<K, V>

```kotlin
fun <T> setOf(vararg elements: T): Set<T>
```
Returns a new read-only set of given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> mutableSetOf(vararg elements: T): MutableSet<T>
```
Returns a new MutableSet with the given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> arrayOf(vararg elements: T): Array<T>
```
Returns an array of given elements.

```kotlin
fun intArrayOf(vararg elements: Int): IntArray
```
Returns an IntArray of given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> T.let(block: (T) -> R): R
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` value as argument and returns its result. Often used for null checks.

completions.label: run
completions.detail: fun <T, R> T.run(block: T.() -> R): R

documentation.value:
```kotlin
fun <T, R> T.run(block: T.() -> R): R
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` as receiver and returns its result.

completions.label: with
completions.detail: fun <T, R> with(receiver: T, block: T.() -> R): R

documentation.value:
```kotlin
fun <T, R> with(receiver: T, block: T.() -> R): R
```
Calls the specified function block with the given receiver as `this` and returns its result.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.apply(block: T.() -> Unit): T
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` as receiver and returns `this`. Used for object configuration.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.also(block: (T) -> Unit): T
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` as argument and returns `this`. Used for side effects.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.takeIf(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): T?
```
Returns `this` if it satisfies the predicate, otherwise null.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.takeUnless(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): T?
```
Returns `this` if it does NOT satisfy the predicate, otherwise null.

```kotlin
fun repeat(times: Int, action: (Int) -> Unit)
```
Executes the given action the specified number of times.

```kotlin

```
Always throws NotImplementedError with an optional reason.

Boolean type with two possible values: true and false.
16-bit Unicode character type.
Immutable sequence of characters.
The type with only one value: Unit. Corresponds to void in Java.
Type that has no instances. Used for functions that never return (e.g., throw).
Root of the Kotlin class hierarchy. Every class has Any as a supertype.
Represents an array. Use arrayOf() to create.
Read-only ordered collection of elements.
Mutable ordered collection supporting add/remove.
Read-only collection of key-value pairs.
Mutable collection of key-value pairs.

Mutable collection of unique elements.

completions.label: Pair

documentation.value: Represents a generic pair of two values. Create with `to` infix function.

completions.label: Triple
completions.detail: data class Triple<out A, out B, out C>

documentation.value: Represents a triple of three values.
A sequence of values that are lazily evaluated.
Classes that inherit from this interface can be iterated over.
A generic collection of elements. Provides read-only access.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.forEach(action: (T) -> Unit)
```
Performs the given action on each element.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> Iterable<T>.map(transform: (T) -> R): List<R>
```
Returns a list containing the results of applying the given transform to each element.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.filter(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): List<T>
```
Returns a list containing only elements matching the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> Iterable<T>.flatMap(transform: (T) -> Iterable<R>): List<R>
```
Returns a single list of all elements yielded from the transform applied to each element.

Accumulates value starting with the first element and applying operation from left to right.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.first(): T
```
Returns the first element. Throws if collection is empty.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.firstOrNull(): T?
```
Returns the first element, or null if collection is empty.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.any(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): Boolean
```
Returns true if at least one element matches the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.all(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): Boolean
```
Returns true if all elements match the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.none(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): Boolean
```
Returns true if no elements match the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.count(): Int
```
Returns the number of elements in the collection.

```kotlin
fun Iterable<Int>.sum(): Int
```
Returns the sum of all elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.joinToString(separator: CharSequence = ", "): String
```
Creates a string from all the elements with the given separator.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.toList(): List<T>
```
Returns a List containing all elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.toMutableList(): MutableList<T>
```
Returns a MutableList containing all elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.toSet(): Set<T>
```
Returns a Set containing all distinct elements.

Represents a compiled regular expression. Use Regex(pattern) or "pattern".toRegex().
Encapsulates a successful or failed result with value or exception.
Interface for types with a natural ordering.
Represents a value with lazy initialization.
Superclass for all numeric types: Int, Long, Short, Byte, Float, Double.
Base class for all errors and exceptions.
Base class for all exceptions.
Thrown when an illegal argument is passed to a function.
Thrown when a method is called at an illegal or inappropriate time.
